UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information within the application, linked to their shopping activity. This entity specifically excludes authentication-related data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This ID is typically provided by an external authentication system and serves as the primary key.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The primary email address of the user, used for communication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a single shisha product available for sale in the Crimson Coals online shop. This includes all details necessary for display and management.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The official name of the shisha product.

description.description: A comprehensive, rich description of the product, potentially generated by an AI assistant for administrators.

price.description: The current selling price of the product.

imageUrl.description: The URL to the primary high-resolution image of the product.

category.description: The category to which the product belongs (e.g., 'Tobacco', 'Charcoals', 'Accessories').

brand.description: The brand name of the product.

stockQuantity.description: The current number of units of this product available in inventory.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the product was first added to the catalog.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the product information was last modified.

ShoppingCart.title: ShoppingCart
ShoppingCart.description: Represents a user's active shopping cart, containing items they have selected for potential purchase. Each user has one active cart.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ShoppingCart entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this shopping cart. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 ShoppingCart)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the shopping cart was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the shopping cart was last modified (e.g., items added, removed, or quantities changed).

CartItem.title: CartItem
CartItem.description: Represents a specific product and its quantity as part of a user's shopping cart. This links products to a cart.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CartItem entity.

cartId.description: Reference to the ShoppingCart this item belongs to. (Relationship: ShoppingCart 1:N CartItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product that this cart item represents. (Relationship: Product 1:N CartItem)

quantity.description: The number of units of the product currently in the cart.

priceAtTimeOfAddition.description: The price of the product when it was added to the cart, capturing the price at that specific moment.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a completed transaction by a user, including order details, shipping, and billing information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who placed this order. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: Timestamp indicating when the order was successfully placed.

totalAmount.description: The final monetary amount of the order, including all items, taxes, and shipping.

status.description: The current fulfillment status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Processing', 'Shipped', 'Delivered', 'Cancelled').

shippingAddressStreet.description: The street address for shipping the order.

shippingAddressCity.description: The city for shipping the order.

shippingAddressState.description: The state or province for shipping the order.

shippingAddressZip.description: The postal code for shipping the order.

shippingAddressCountry.description: The country for shipping the order.

billingAddressStreet.description: The street address for billing the order.

billingAddressCity.description: The city for billing the order.

billingAddressState.description: The state or province for billing the order.

billingAddressZip.description: The postal code for billing the order.

billingAddressCountry.description: The country for billing the order.

paymentMethod.description: The payment method used for the order (e.g., 'Credit Card', 'PayPal'). Note: Sensitive payment information is not stored here.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was last updated (e.g., status change).

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single product and its quantity that was part of a completed order. This preserves the product details at the time of purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product that was purchased. (Relationship: Product 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The number of units of this specific product purchased in the order.

unitPrice.description: The price of the product at the exact moment it was purchased, ensuring historical pricing accuracy for the order.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Each document's ID matches the `userId` in the path. Access is restricted to the owning user (`request.auth.uid == userId`).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, typically from Firebase Authentication, matching the UserProfile.id.

definition.description: The public catalog of all available shisha products. Readable by anyone. Write access is restricted to administrators (verified via `/roles_admin/{adminUid}`).

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique ID of the product.

definition.description: Stores a user's active shopping cart. Each document includes `userId` for authorization independence. Access is restricted to the owning user (`request.auth.uid == userId`).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this shopping cart.

params.name: cartId
params.description: The unique ID of the shopping cart. For a single active cart, this might be a static identifier like 'active'.

definition.description: Items within a specific user's shopping cart. Each document *must include a denormalized 'userId' field* (copied from the parent ShoppingCart or path) for authorization independence. Access is restricted to the owning user (`request.auth.uid == userId`).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this cart item (denormalized).

params.name: cartId
params.description: The ID of the shopping cart this item belongs to.

params.name: cartItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the cart item.

definition.description: Stores a user's completed orders. Each document includes `userId` for authorization independence. Access is restricted to the owning user (`request.auth.uid == userId`).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who placed this order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique ID of the order.

definition.description: Individual items included in a specific user's order. Each document *must include a denormalized 'userId' field* (copied from the parent Order or path) for authorization independence. Access is restricted to the owning user (`request.auth.uid == userId`).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who placed this order item (denormalized).

params.name: orderId
params.description: The ID of the order this item belongs to.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the order item.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document at `/roles_admin/{request.auth.uid}` grants administrative privileges for actions like product management. No specific schema, just document existence is checked.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The unique ID of a user designated as an administrator. The document itself may be empty or contain minimal data.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling to ensure robust security, scalability, and debuggability for the Crimson Coals online shop. Each collection serves a distinct purpose with a homogeneous security posture.

**Authorization Independence:**
*   User-specific collections (`/users/{userId}`, `/users/{userId}/shoppingCarts/{cartId}`, `/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}`) embed the `userId` directly in the path. Furthermore, the `UserProfile`, `ShoppingCart`, and `Order` entities explicitly include a `userId` field (or their `id` matches the `userId` path parameter). This allows security rules to check `request.auth.uid == userId` (from the path wildcard) or `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without performing `get()` operations on parent documents. This prevents cascading rule dependencies and enables atomic write operations.
*   For nested subcollections (`/users/{userId}/shoppingCarts/{cartId}/cartItems/{cartItemId}` and `/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}/orderItems/{orderItemId}`), the `CartItem` and `OrderItem` documents *must* denormalize the `userId` from their respective parent `ShoppingCart` or `Order` during creation. This is critical for authorization independence, allowing security rules for `cartItems` and `orderItems` to directly verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without traversing up the hierarchy using `get()`. This makes rules simpler to write, reason about, and debug.

**Query as Permission (QAPs) Support:**
*   The `/products` collection is designed for public read access, allowing any user (authenticated or not) to list and view available products without filtering. Write access is restricted to authenticated administrators, verified by the existence of a document in `/roles_admin/{uid}`.
*   All user-specific data (profiles, shopping carts, orders, and their respective items) are stored under `/users/{userId}/...` paths. This path-based ownership inherently supports QAPs. When a user queries `/users/$(request.auth.uid)/shoppingCarts`, for example, the `request.auth.uid` matches the `userId` wildcard, ensuring they can only retrieve their own data. Security rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, preventing unauthorized users from listing or accessing another user's private information.
*   The `roles_admin` collection facilitates admin role-based access by using existence checks (`get(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)).exists()`), enabling secure administrative actions while supporting QAPs by only allowing specific UIDs to exist as admin roles.

**Addressing the User Request:**
*   "saves it in my list": This is directly handled by the `/users/{userId}/shoppingCarts/{cartId}/cartItems/{cartItemId}` path. Each user has their own dedicated shopping cart where items can be saved, modified, and removed, secured by `userId` ownership.
*   "and shop": The `/products/{productId}` collection represents the central product catalog or "shop". Users add items from this publicly viewable collection into their private shopping carts. This clear separation between public catalog and private user lists ensures both user requirements are met securely and scalably.
